_meta.description: Labeled seed corpus for the scheduled-daily-inbox-briefing summary-quality eval (#1608 feature, #1951 tracking). Each case is one inbox slice the daily briefing summarizes, plus a rubric of the high-priority threads it MUST surface and the noise it MUST NOT flag as high-priority. Scored by gaia.eval.briefing_quality: the REAL scheduled-briefing path (gaia_agent_email.briefing.run_briefing_job -> pre_scan_inbox_impl) produces the email_pre_scan envelope over a FakeGmailBackend seeded with the inbox slice (read-only, nothing sent/archived), an LLM judge scores it against the inbox + rubric on faithfulness / must-include recall / hallucination-free / grouping, and the aggregate briefing_approval_rate is compared to briefing_gate_thresholds.json in report mode.

case_schema.scenario: str — short label for the inbox situation this case exercises
case_schema.inbox: list[{from: str, subject: str, body: str}] (>=3) — the inbox slice the briefing summarizes; the ONLY ground truth for faithfulness + hallucination checks
case_schema.rubric: {must_surface: list[str] (non-empty), must_not_surface: list[str] (may be empty), notes?: str} — must_surface = high-priority threads that must appear in urgent/actionable (drives must-include recall); must_not_surface = noise that must NOT be flagged high-priority

briefing-001.scenario: prod incident + review request buried under newsletters

inbox.subject: [P1] Checkout API 5xx error rate above threshold
inbox.body: A P1 incident has been triggered for the Checkout API. Error rate is at 34% (threshold 2%) for the last 12 minutes. On-call has been paged. Ack this alert or it escalates to the secondary on-call in 5 minutes.

inbox.subject: Need your sign-off on the Q3 budget by EOD
inbox.body: Hi — finance needs the Q3 budget locked today. I've attached the final numbers; can you reply with your approval before 5pm? Nothing else is blocking the submission.

inbox.subject: Today in tech: 10 startups to watch
inbox.body: Your daily roundup of the biggest stories in tech. This morning: a new AI unicorn, chip supply updates, and more.

inbox.subject: Your order has shipped
inbox.body: Your recent order of USB-C cables has shipped and will arrive Thursday. Track your package here.

inbox.subject: You have 3 new connection requests
inbox.body: See who wants to connect with you this week. Grow your network.

must_surface: The PagerDuty P1 checkout-API incident (time-critical, on-call escalation)
Dana Kim's Q3 budget sign-off request due EOD today
rubric.notes: The incident should read as the single most urgent item; the budget sign-off is a same-day action, not an emergency.

briefing-002.scenario: two competing deadlines, one genuine FYI

inbox.subject: Signature required: vendor NDA before tomorrow's call
inbox.body: The Globex NDA needs your signature before the 9am call tomorrow or we cannot share the roadmap. DocuSign link inside — it takes two minutes.

inbox.subject: Can you review the launch blog post today?
inbox.body: Marketing wants to publish the launch post tomorrow morning. Could you review the draft today and flag anything inaccurate? Draft is in the shared doc.

inbox.subject: Scheduled maintenance this weekend
inbox.body: Heads up: VPN will be down for maintenance Saturday 2-4am. No action needed; just avoid scheduling anything critical in that window.

must_surface: The Legal NDA signature needed before tomorrow's 9am call
Priya's request to review the launch blog post today
must_not_surface: The IT weekend-maintenance FYI (explicitly says no action needed)
rubric.notes: Both surfaced items are time-boxed actions; the IT notice is purely informational.

briefing-003.scenario: mostly noise with a single real action item

inbox.subject: Your 2026 Wrapped is here
inbox.body: See the songs and artists that defined your year. Tap to view your personalized Wrapped.

inbox.subject: Stories for you
inbox.body: Based on your reading history, here are today's recommended stories across product, design, and engineering.

inbox.subject: Contract renewal — decision needed this week
inbox.body: Our current contract lapses Friday. Can you confirm whether you want to renew at the same terms? If I don't hear back by Thursday we'll have to pause service.

inbox.subject: Your weekly writing stats
inbox.body: You were more productive than 78% of Grammarly users this week. See your streak and insights.

must_surface: Marcus Lee's contract-renewal decision needed by Thursday/Friday
rubric.notes: Only one thread carries a real deadline; the rest are promotional and must not be flagged high-priority.

briefing-004.scenario: phishing attempt mixed with legitimate mail

inbox.subject: Unusual sign-in — verify your account within 24 hours
inbox.body: We detected an unusual sign-in to your Microsoft account. Verify your identity immediately at the link below or your account will be suspended. Enter your password to confirm it's you.

inbox.subject: Slides for Thursday's board meeting — feedback?
inbox.body: Draft board slides attached. Could you take a pass by Wednesday? Mainly want your eyes on the revenue section.

inbox.subject: New courses added to your recommendations
inbox.body: We've added new machine-learning courses we think you'll like. Enroll now and start learning.

must_surface: The suspected phishing / account-verification scam email (user must review, not act on it blindly)
Sofia's request to review the board slides by Wednesday
rubric.notes: A phishing lure must be surfaced for the user's attention (never silently archived), and never summarized as a legitimate security request the user should comply with.

inbox.subject: Dinner Sunday?
inbox.body: Hi honey, are you free for dinner this Sunday? Your sister is coming too. No rush, just let me know when you can. Love you.

inbox.subject: Build failed on main — your commit
inbox.body: The main branch build is red after your last merge. The email-agent test suite failed. Please fix or revert so the team isn't blocked.

inbox.subject: Reminder: 1:1 with manager at 2pm
inbox.body: This is a reminder for your event '1:1 with manager' today at 2:00 PM.

inbox.subject: What's new in Notion this month
inbox.body: Check out the latest features we shipped: improved search, new templates, and more.

must_surface: The failed main-branch build caused by the user's commit (blocking the team)
rubric.notes: The broken build is the one blocking action. The personal dinner note and the calendar reminder are legitimate but low-priority; they must not be distorted into work emergencies, and the personal note must never be summarized inaccurately.

briefing-006.scenario: escalating customer thread plus routine approvals

inbox.subject: Escalation: data export broken for 3 days, considering churn
inbox.body: This is the third day our nightly data export has failed and support has not resolved it. We are evaluating alternatives. I need someone senior to call me today with a plan, or we will start migrating off the platform.

inbox.subject: 2 expense reports awaiting your approval
inbox.body: You have two expense reports from your team awaiting approval. They are within policy. Approve or reject at your convenience.

inbox.subject: Events happening near you this week
inbox.body: Discover tech meetups and networking events in your area this week.

must_surface: The Big Customer churn-risk escalation demanding a senior call today
The two expense reports awaiting the user's approval
rubric.notes: The churn escalation is the clear top priority; the expense approvals are a genuine but routine action, not an emergency.

briefing-007.scenario: all-informational morning with one soft ask

inbox.subject: Recap: this week's all-hands recording
inbox.body: In case you missed it, the all-hands recording and slides are now available on the intranet. No action needed.

inbox.subject: When you get a chance — thoughts on the API naming?
inbox.body: No rush at all, but when you have a spare moment this week I'd love your take on whether we call it 'workspaces' or 'projects'. Happy to grab coffee to discuss.

inbox.subject: Weekly digest: activity in your repositories
inbox.body: Here's what happened across your repositories this week: 12 pull requests merged, 4 new issues opened.

inbox.subject: Your Tuesday afternoon trip receipt
inbox.body: Thanks for riding. Your trip total was $18.40. View the full receipt and trip details.

must_surface: Alex Chen's low-urgency request for input on API naming (a real ask, even if 'no rush')
rubric.notes: Alex's message is the only one asking anything of the user; the briefing should surface it as a light actionable, not inflate it into something urgent, and must not fabricate deadlines it doesn't contain.

briefing-008.scenario: duplicate-looking subjects that are actually distinct threads

inbox.subject: Re: Onboarding docs — one blocker
inbox.body: Thanks for the docs! One blocker: the API key section links to a 404. New hires start Monday, so could you fix the link before then?

inbox.subject: Onboarding docs — separate question about access
inbox.body: Unrelated to the doc thread: can you also grant the two new hires read access to the analytics dashboard? Not urgent, sometime before Monday is fine.

inbox.subject: Your Jira weekly summary
inbox.body: Here's a summary of your Jira activity this week across boards and sprints.

must_surface: Jordan's broken API-key link blocking Monday's new-hire onboarding
Jordan's separate request to grant the new hires analytics-dashboard access before Monday
rubric.notes: Two genuinely distinct asks from the same sender with similar subjects — the briefing must not collapse them into one item or drop one; both share a Monday deadline.

briefing-009.scenario: time-zone-sensitive meeting request plus a receipt

inbox.subject: Can we move our sync 2 hours earlier? My end is late-night
inbox.body: Our recurring sync lands at 11pm my time. Could we shift it two hours earlier going forward? If today works for a one-off earlier slot, I'm free from 3pm your time. Let me know today so I can plan.

inbox.subject: Receipt for your subscription payment
inbox.body: Your monthly subscription of $49.00 was charged successfully. This is your receipt; no action is required.

inbox.subject: New in Figma: dev mode improvements
inbox.body: We've shipped improvements to dev mode. See what's new and how to use it.

must_surface: Yuki's request to reschedule the recurring sync, needing a reply today
must_not_surface: The Stripe subscription receipt (no action required)
rubric.notes: The reschedule ask needs a same-day reply; the briefing must summarize the specific time request faithfully (2 hours earlier / 3pm your time) without inventing a confirmed new time.

briefing-010.scenario: security advisory plus a genuine deadline and a promo

inbox.subject: Action required: rotate your API tokens by Friday
inbox.body: Following the dependency advisory, all engineers must rotate their personal API tokens by end of day Friday. Instructions are on the security wiki. Tokens not rotated will be revoked automatically.

inbox.subject: Interview feedback needed for tomorrow's debrief
inbox.body: Could you submit your written feedback for yesterday's candidate before tomorrow's 10am debrief? We can't make a decision without it.

inbox.subject: 50% off your next 3 orders
inbox.body: Limited time: get 50% off your next three orders. Tap to claim your offer before it expires.

must_surface: The security team's mandatory API-token rotation by Friday
Rachel's interview-feedback request needed before tomorrow's 10am debrief
rubric.notes: Both surfaced items are hard deadlines with consequences; the briefing must keep their due dates accurate (Friday for rotation, tomorrow 10am for feedback).
